name: The 19th Hole at the Gauntlet
about: The 19th Hole at the Gauntlet is a golf simulator venue located within a semi-public golf club. It features a state-of-the-art Trackman4 Indoor Simulator bay available for hourly rentals. The venue is part of the Gauntlet Golf Club, which includes a full-service bar and grille, dining areas, and a large practice facility. The simulator bay is conveniently situated next to the 19th Hole Bar and Grill, offering a social atmosphere for both practice and events. The facility provides access to hundreds of golf courses with detailed shot analytics.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to the industry-leading Trackman4 simulator for precise shot tracking and analysis
Prime location adjacent to the 19th Hole Bar and Grill, offering a social and relaxed atmosphere
Ability to play on over 130 world-renowned courses with high accuracy
Suitable for groups with hourly rentals hosting up to 8 players

food_and_drink.notes: Full-service bar and grille with a grill serving fresh bites and weekly specials
Fully stocked bar with local craft beers, whiskey, bourbon, and scotch options

simulator_hardware.notes: Industry-leading radar launch monitor with shot analytics

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, players should bring their own golf clubs to use in the simulator bay.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No, the venue has a full-service bar and grille on-site. Outside food and drinks are not permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The time to complete 18 holes can vary but typically ranges from 2-3 hours depending on simulator availability and pace of play.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is family-friendly and offers programs like the PGA Jr. League, making it suitable for juniors and youth golfers.
